title: NotebookLM + Claude Code: Complete Automation Guide
shortDescription: Build an automated research pipeline connecting Claude Code to Google's NotebookLM. Scrape YouTube, generate analysis, and create infographics with a single command.
contentHtml:
This comprehensive guide shows you how to build an automated research pipeline that connects Claude Code to Google's NotebookLM. Give it a topic, and it automatically scrapes YouTube videos, analyzes trends, and generates professional deliverables like infographics, podcasts, and study guides—all without manual input.

Originally created by Steve Arneson and powered by Teng Lin's unofficial NotebookLM Python library, this system is perfect for agency owners, content creators, and anyone who needs to stay ahead of industry trends without spending hours on manual research.

What This System Does

The pipeline gives you four core capabilities that work together seamlessly:

  • YouTube Research: Automated scraping of video metadata (titles, views, authors, duration, URLs) based on any search query
  • Cross-Source Analysis: NotebookLM processes all sources simultaneously for deep cross-referencing and pattern detection
  • Podcast Briefings: Auto-generated audio overviews and summaries you can listen to on the go
  • Visual Deliverables: Create infographics, slide decks, flashcards, and study guides on demand

"Use the yt-research skill to find the 25 latest trending videos on [YOUR TOPIC]. Once we have those videos, send them to NotebookLM. Give me its analysis on top findings, then have NotebookLM create an infographic in a handwritten/chalkboard style."

— Example research command (works with any topic)

The Complete Setup Process

The guide breaks down setup into one simple prompt you paste into Claude Code. No manual Python installations, no complex configuration files—just paste and accept the changes. Here's what happens:

  1. YouTube Research Skill: Claude Code creates a custom Python skill using yt-dlp for video scraping
  2. NotebookLM Integration: Installs the unofficial API and sets up commands for notebook creation, source uploads, and deliverable generation
  3. Authentication Guide: Step-by-step instructions for logging into NotebookLM (including the critical browser support fix missing from other guides)
  4. Readiness Confirmation: Claude Code verifies everything works before your first research run

NotebookLM Authentication (The Working Method)

The original NotebookLM guides have incomplete login instructions. This guide provides the tested method that actually works:

# Install with browser support (critical step)
pip install "notebooklm-py[browser]"

# Install Chromium for Playwright
playwright install chromium

# Open SEPARATE terminal and login
notebooklm login

# Verify authentication
notebooklm auth check --test
notebooklm list

Key insight: Playwright is only needed for the login command. Every other operation uses standard HTTP, so you can copy your authentication token to servers without installing Playwright there.

What's in the Full PDF

  • Complete setup prompt (copy-paste ready)
  • 4-step workflow from installation to first research run
  • NotebookLM login fix with browser support (works on Mac, Windows, Linux)
  • Complete CLI command reference (40+ commands)
  • Troubleshooting guide for common errors
  • Use cases for agency owners and content creators
  • Manual/headless authentication methods
  • Debug commands and logging techniques
  • Rate limit handling and retry strategies

Compiled by Krish Chhatrala based on Steve Arneson's system and the notebooklm-py library by Teng Lin. This is a community project using undocumented Google APIs—best for prototypes, research, and personal projects.
